metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the open-ended store-scan in AC5 with a concrete proving command (e.g. a one-liner query against the store), a defined pass condition (zero whitespace-only titles found → no migration needed), and an explicit fallback action (e.g. 'if any found, open a migration sub-task before merging') so the criterion is unambiguously completable.
findings.finding: AC5 contains placeholder language ('confirm whether any stored tickets have whitespace-only titles — a one-off store scan') that defers a real design decision to the executor: it specifies neither the tool/command to run the scan, nor what to do if whitespace-only titles are found. As written, an executor cannot proceed without asking 'how do I scan?', 'what constitutes a pass?', and 'do we migrate, reject at read, or grandfather?'. It is also outcome-vague (F1) and has no proving command (E6).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC5 scan bullet with: 'Run `python -m rebar._commands.scan_titles --whitespace-only` (or equivalent store query) before merging; if output is empty the AC passes; if non-empty, open a migration sub-task and block this PR until resolved.' Adjust the command to whatever the actual store access pattern is.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving check for AC6, e.g. `grep -q 'whitespace' CHANGELOG.md` and `python -m rebar --help | grep -q 'whitespace'` (adjusted to actual paths), and name the specific files that must be changed.
findings.finding: AC6 ('User-facing docs updated: CLI help text / API reference and a CHANGELOG entry') has no proving command and no concrete artifact specification. `make lint && make typecheck && make test` do not verify that help text was changed or that a CHANGELOG entry exists. The criterion is effort-framed ('updated') with no observable outcome to assert.
findings.suggested_fix: Specify: 'CHANGELOG.md gains an entry under the current version mentioning whitespace-title rejection (verify: `grep -c whitespace CHANGELOG.md` returns ≥1); the CLI `--help` output or docstring for the title argument mentions rejection of whitespace-only values (verify: `python -m rebar create --help | grep -qi whitespace`).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete resolution branch to AC item 5: specify what to do if the store scan finds whitespace-only titles (e.g., migrate them, log a warning, block the release, or document a data-fix script) so the 'no migration silently required' guarantee is actually enforced.
findings.finding: AC item 5 requires a one-off store scan to confirm whether existing tickets have whitespace-only titles, but the plan specifies no resolution path if the scan finds any — leaving an open 'what if migration IS required?' branch unaddressed. The Scope section also omits any scan tool, script, or migration step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, item 5: 'Breaking-change handling … confirm whether any stored tickets have whitespace-only titles (a one-off store scan) so no migration is silently required.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC item 5 to: 'Run a one-off store scan; if zero whitespace-only titles are found, proceed; if any are found, apply [chosen remediation: auto-strip and re-save / manual data-fix script / block release until cleaned] before deploying the validation tightening.' Add the scan script path to Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete rollback/back-out plan (e.g., a one-line revert, a feature flag, or a phase-1-warn/phase-2-reject sequence) AND add a conditional remediation branch to AC 6: 'if the store scan finds whitespace-only-titled records, run migration M (or document operator action O) before enforcing rejection.'
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges the breaking change and mentions 'Document a back-out/expand-contract note,' but provides no concrete rollback/back-out plan in the plan itself, and — critically — specifies no action to take if the one-off store scan discovers existing whitespace-only-titled tickets in the store. If such records exist, they become unreachable or create inconsistency post-deploy with no stated remediation path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 6: 'Breaking-change handling: … Document a back-out/expand-contract note, and confirm whether any stored tickets have whitespace-only titles (a one-off store scan) so no migration is silently required.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC 6 to two explicit sub-steps: (a) run the store scan before deploying; (b) if count > 0, either run a migration script that replaces whitespace-only titles with a placeholder (e.g., '(untitled)') OR gate the rejection behind a feature flag until migration is complete. Add a rollback note: 'to revert, revert commit X; no schema change is required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name BOTH validation sites that need updating: `create_core` line 112 (`if not title:` → `if not title.strip():`) AND `edit_core` line 380 (`if value == ""` → `if not value.strip():`) — AC-1 requires both create and edit paths to reject whitespace-only titles.
findings.finding: The plan scopes `composer.py` for the whitespace fix but only calls out line 112 (`create_core`'s `if not title:` guard). The EDIT path has a separate, structurally different guard at line 380 (`if value == ""`) that must also be updated to `if not value.strip():`, yet the plan never explicitly names this second site. An implementer following the plan could fix only the create guard and ship with edit still accepting whitespace-only titles, violating AC-1.
findings.location: Scope section / composer.py line 380 (edit_core title guard)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What or Scope section: 'Two guard sites require updating: `create_core` line 112 and `edit_core` line 380 (the edit guard currently checks `value == ""`, which misses whitespace-only values).'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `rebar idea` CLI / `rebar.idea()` library / MCP `create_idea` to the enumerated affected surfaces in Scope and AC #5 (breaking-change note) and AC #6 (docs/CHANGELOG), and include at least one whitespace-title rejection test for the idea surface.
findings.finding: `idea_cli` / `rebar.idea()` / MCP `create_idea` are unenumerated consumers of `create_core` that accept a `title` argument. After the fix a whitespace-only title on these surfaces is newly rejected, but none are listed in the Scope, AC, or docs-update section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the Scope section to explicitly list the doc files (CLI help, API reference, CHANGELOG) and the migration artifact (store scan / back-out note) that AC items 6 and 7 require, OR move those requirements to a follow-up story and remove them from this plan's ACs.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists only two artifacts (`src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` and `tests/interfaces/facades/`), but AC item 6 requires a 'back-out/expand-contract note' and a 'one-off store scan' for whitespace-only titles, and AC item 7 requires 'CLI help text / API reference' updates and a 'CHANGELOG entry' — none of these doc/migration artifacts appear in Scope. This sends the implementer in two directions: the Scope boundary says 'only these two files,' but the ACs mandate additional artifacts.
findings.location: Scope section vs AC items 6 and 7
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `docs/` (CHANGELOG + API reference/help text update) and a one-off migration script or manual verification step for existing stored tickets with whitespace-only titles. Alternatively, if these are pre-conditions or follow-ons, note them explicitly as out-of-scope with a linked follow-up.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for keeping validation inline vs. extracting a shared `_validate_title()` helper — even one sentence ('inline is sufficient given there are only two call sites and no further rules are planned') satisfies G6's rationale requirement.
findings.finding: The plan gives a clear business rationale (Why section) for rejecting whitespace-only titles, but provides no positive rationale for the chosen code-level mechanism — specifically, why the validation is kept inline at each guard site rather than extracted into a shared `_validate_title(title)` helper called from both `create_core` and `edit_core`. Without this rationale, the two-site inline approach appears accidental rather than deliberate, and future guard additions (e.g. `idea_cli`'s direct arg parsing) may not follow the same pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the rejection-identity contract (same message substring, facade-appropriate surface) in a persistent artifact — CLAUDE.md, a design doc, or a lightweight ADR — so future facade authors can discover and uphold it.
findings.finding: The rejection-identity contract — 'all three facades reject with the SAME human-readable message substring on their facade-appropriate surface' — is a novel cross-facade architectural decision introduced by this plan. It is defined only in the plan's ephemeral 'What' section and not captured in any persistent artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md, design doc). Future facade authors (e.g., a fourth facade or a maintainer refactoring error messages) have no durable record of this invariant, creating a silent drift risk.
findings.location: What section — 'Rejection identity (across facades)' definition; AC line 4 (corpus round-trip assertion)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/decisions/facade-rejection-identity.md` (or an entry in CLAUDE.md under a 'Cross-facade contracts' section) that records: (1) the invariant definition verbatim, (2) the rationale (consistent UX across surfaces), (3) the U+2192 exception, and (4) a pointer to the relevant test file. Reference this doc in a code comment near the shared validation logic in `composer.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `session_log.start()` (and the `rebar session-log start --summary=` CLI / `start_session_log()` library surface) as an affected consumer of `create_core`; add a corresponding test case and include it in the AC #5 breaking-change note and AC #6 docs update.
findings.finding: `session_log.start()` is an unenumerated consumer of `create_core` that passes a user-supplied `summary` as the title. After the fix, a whitespace-only `--summary=` value (truthy, passes the `summary or _DEFAULT_TITLE` guard) would flow into `create_core` and be newly rejected — a behavioral change on an undocumented surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: `src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py` (affected consumer — no code change needed, but test and docs coverage required for the `--summary=<whitespace>` rejection case). Add a test in `tests/interfaces/facades/` for this surface and include it in the CHANGELOG entry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC2 whether the 255-character limit is measured in Unicode code points, UTF-8 bytes, or another unit, and confirm this matches the existing enforcement in `composer.py`.
findings.finding: The `>255`-character title rejection limit in AC2 is specified without clarifying whether '255' is measured in Unicode code points, UTF-8 bytes, or UTF-16 code units. In a Unicode-aware codebase handling emoji and non-BMP characters, a 256-character string of emoji is ≫256 bytes; the existing code's enforcement mechanism determines which unit is actually checked, but the plan does not state the unit, leaving the test author to guess.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical, e.g. 'a >255 Unicode code-point title' (or 'UTF-8 byte') to match the actual enforcement in `composer.py` (~line 255 area), and use a fixture string that is unambiguous for that unit (e.g. 256 ASCII chars for a code-point limit, or 256 × U+0041 bytes for a byte limit).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
